milestone_brief: Lift CONTEXT.md §9 non-goal "No 3D mesh export" — add File → Export Mesh… menu action that lets the user save the current surface to STL, OBJ, or PLY via PyVista's mesh.save(...). Scope: app.py adds a File menu (currently no File menu exists — the only menu bar item is Theme) with one action "Export Mesh…" wired to a _on_export_mesh handler that opens QFileDialog.getSaveFileName with a format filter ("STL files (*.stl);;OBJ files (*.obj);;PLY files (*.ply)"), invokes self._raw_mesh.save(path) to persist (PyVista routes by extension), and surfaces success/failure via statusBar().showMessage. AI-15 attestation: the EXPORTED mesh is self._raw_mesh (the unclipped marching-cubes / Flying-Edges + Taubin output) — NOT the domain-clipped mesh — because the clip is a viewing convention not a mesh-fidelity choice; the user gets the canonical surface for downstream analysis. Action is disabled when self._raw_mesh is None (no surface yet rendered). AI-9: synchronous mesh.save() runs on the GUI thread but is fast (~10-50ms for the ~100k-vertex meshes this app produces) — no need for QThread; if a future variety produces multi-million-vertex meshes this gates the spinner-already-shipped pattern. Tests: pure source-grep (AI-2) asserting the File menu action exists, the handler invokes mesh.save, the format filter strings are present, and the disabled-when-no-mesh guard is present. Acceptance: user can render any surface, click File → Export Mesh…, pick a path with .stl/.obj/.ply extension, and the file is written.
research_synthesis: File → Export Mesh… (Ctrl+E) action via QFileDialog → mesh.save. Filter: STL/OBJ/PLY. Save target: self._raw_mesh (unclipped). Disabled at construction; enabled on _on_mesh_ready success; re-disabled on error AND on _on_variety_changed placeholder branch (load-bearing — _clear_actor does NOT reset _raw_mesh). Pre-validate extension (Linux/macOS don't auto-append). Broad except Exception (PyVista raises ValueError on bad ext, PermissionError, FileNotFoundError). AI-9: sync save ~10-50ms; AI-15: existing per-variety tooltips carry the shadow/birational caveats — no new claims. 3 files, ~141 LOC.
